_comment: Radio definitions for the KDH bootloader flasher. Community contributions welcome — add your radio and submit a PR. Set 'tested' to true only after confirming a successful flash.

radios.name: BTECH BF-F8HP Pro
radios.bootloader_keys: SK1 + SK2 (top and bottom side buttons, not PTT). Volume to max. Press cable connector firmly into radio and hold pressure during entire flash.
radios.notes: Confirmed working on V0.53 firmware. The K1 connector may need firm pressure to maintain the data return contact on the 2.5mm ring. Keep steady pressure on the cable plug during the entire flash process.

radios.name: Baofeng UV-25 Plus / UV-25 Pro
radios.notes: Uses same KDH bootloader. Firmware available from baofengradio.com. Do not use if your UV-25 does not have GPS — it will cause the radio to malfunction.

radios.name: Radtel RT-470
radios.notes: Uses WLT2000 CPS. KDH bootloader with .kdhx firmware. This URL is for the NEW PCB (V2.12A, full band). Old PCB (10W) uses different firmware — see radtels.com.

radios.name: Radtel RT-490
radios.notes: Shares JJCC JC-8629 PCB. KDH bootloader. This URL is for the old PCB (v1.03). Newer PCB versions with channel name editing available on radtels.com.

radios.name: Other KDH Radio
radios.bootloader_keys: Check your radio's manual for bootloader key combination
radios.notes: Generic option for any radio using the KDH bootloader. Browse for a .kdhx firmware file you have downloaded from your manufacturer. Many radios based on KeDiHeng designs (Abbree, Hamgeek, Socotran, JJCC, etc.) use this bootloader.
